meta.purpose: Define growth stages and measurable indicators for management skill development.
meta.load_condition: Load when the user asks how to improve management skills or measure leadership progress.

stages.name: Person Blamer
stages.description: Sees execution problems as individual failures. Intervenes by coaching, warning, or replacing individuals. Does not distinguish between system causes and person causes.
indicators: Default reaction to missed deadlines is 'we need to have a conversation'
Uses banned terms: lazy, unmotivated, doesn't care, needs to step up
Same problems recur across different people in the same role
Team turnover is higher than team performance improvement

stages.name: System-Aware Manager
stages.description: Recognizes that systems produce behavior but still defaults to individual intervention. Can diagnose upstream causes when guided but doesn't systematically check for them before acting.
indicators: Can identify upstream causes in retrospect but not proactively
Sometimes fixes the system but more often addresses individuals
Has begun defining standards but they are still inconsistent
Team performance is improving but manager is still the bottleneck

stages.name: Upstream Diagnostician
stages.description: Defaults to upstream diagnosis before any individual intervention. Invests in standards, visible performance, and psychological safety. The team performs whether the manager is present or not.
indicators: Before any individual conversation about performance, has checked system conditions
Team can self-assess performance against published standards
Problems surface early because team trusts the response
Execution standards are explicit enough that new team members self-calibrate within weeks

stages.name: Management System Designer
stages.description: Designs management systems that produce upstream diagnosticians. Builds organizational infrastructure — hiring standards, onboarding playbooks, performance systems — so that management quality is a property of the organization, not of individual managers.
indicators: New managers in the organization default to upstream diagnosis within their first quarter
Performance consistency across teams is high regardless of manager tenure or style
The organization's management language is precise, standard-based, and self-reinforcing
External hires notice the difference in management quality within their first month

evolution_layers.name: System Recognition
evolution_layers.capability: Ability to see execution problems as outputs of system conditions rather than as individual failures.

evolution_layers.name: Upstream Diagnosis
evolution_layers.capability: Ability to consistently and accurately identify the system conditions producing execution problems before addressing individuals.

evolution_layers.name: Management System Design
evolution_layers.capability: Ability to build organizational systems (hiring, onboarding, standards, feedback) that produce good management at scale.

measurement.what: Percentage of performance conversations that start with system diagnosis rather than person judgment
measurement.how: Review manager one-on-one notes for 20 performance-related conversations. Count how many start with system question versus person accusation.
measurement.threshold: At least 80% of performance conversations begin with system diagnosis.

measurement.what: Average time between problem emergence and manager awareness
measurement.how: For each project issue identified in a quarter, measure days between when the team first knew and when the manager knew.
measurement.threshold: Median surface time under 3 days. No problem surfaces only at deadline.

measurement.what: Percentage of team members who can self-assess their performance against standards
measurement.how: Ask each team member: 'How do you know if you're doing a good job?' Grade answers on whether they reference specific standards versus 'my manager tells me.'
measurement.threshold: At least 80% of team members can cite specific, measurable standards without referencing the manager.
